agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module within an HR software system. Governs how employees accrue, request, consume, and carry over paid leave. This is a payroll-adjacent domain where correctness directly affects employee compensation, labor law compliance, and operational staffing.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued leave days available to an employee. Cannot be negative. Represents earned but unused leave."},{"term":"Accrual","definition":"The process by which an employee earns 1.5 leave days per month worked. Accrual is tied to months of active employment."},{"term":"Leave Request","definition":"A formal submission by an employee to consume accrued leave days for a specific date range. Must be approved by a manager before leave is taken."},{"term":"Medical Leave","definition":"A special category of leave that may be submitted retroactively (within 5 calendar days) provided a doctor's note is attached. Exempt from the 3-working-day advance requirement."},{"term":"Working Days","definition":"Business days excluding weekends and company-recognized holidays. Used to measure the advance notice requirement for non-medical leave requests."},{"term":"Doctor's Note","definition":"A required attachment for retroactive medical leave requests. Without it, a medical leave request submitted after the fact is invalid."},{"term":"Contract End Date","definition":"The date on which an employee's employment contract terminates. Leave requests must not include any dates on or after this boundary."},{"term":"Carry-Over","definition":"The process at calendar year boundary where up to 10 days of unused leave transfer to the next year. Days above 10 are forfeited."},{"term":"Forfeiture","definition":"The permanent loss of accrued leave days exceeding the 10-day carry-over cap at calendar year end."},{"term":"Leave Approval","definition":"A manager's decision to approve a leave request, which then consumes the employee's leave balance."},{"term":"Leave Rejection","definition":"A manager's decision to reject a leave request. Rejected requests do not consume any leave balance."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Employees accrue 1.5 days of leave per month worked.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Accrual rate is a company policy parameter. HR leadership could change it to 2.0 days/month without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"After each completed month of employment, the employee's leave balance increases by exactly 1.5 days."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Leave balance cannot go negative. Leave cannot be taken before it is accrued.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A negative leave balance means an employee consumed leave they never earned — this is a state corruption. The balance is a physical constraint: you cannot spend what does not exist.","testable_assertion":"At no point in time shall an employee's leave balance be less than zero. Any operation that would result in a negative balance must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Non-medical leave requests must be submitted at least 3 working days before the leave start date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The advance notice window is an administrative policy. Management could change it to 5 working days or 1 working day without corrupting data integrity.","testable_assertion":"A non-medical leave request submitted fewer than 3 working days before the requested start date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Medical leave can be submitted retroactively within 5 calendar days of the leave date, provided a doctor's note is attached.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 5-day retroactive window and the doctor's note requirement are policy decisions, not data integrity constraints.","testable_assertion":"A medical leave request for a past date is accepted only if (a) submission is within 5 calendar days of the leave date and (b) a doctor's note is attached. If either condition fails, the request is rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Only a manager can approve or reject a leave request.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Authorization integrity — if a non-manager could approve leave, the approval chain is corrupted. This is a security and data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"The system only permits users with the Manager role (relative to the requesting employee) to transition a leave request to Approved or Rejected status."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Rejected leave does not consume leave balance.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If a rejection consumed balance, the system has silently stolen earned compensation from the employee. This is state corruption.","testable_assertion":"When a leave request transitions to Rejected status, the employee's leave balance remains unchanged from the value before the request was submitted."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A leave request must not span across or beyond an employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"An employee cannot be on leave when they are no longer employed. Allowing this would create a contract-violating state — leave for a non-employee.","testable_assertion":"A leave request where any requested leave day falls on or after the employee's contract end date is rejected at submission time."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"At calendar year end, unused leave above 10 days is forfeited. Up to 10 days carry over.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-day carry-over cap is a company policy parameter. It could be changed to 15 days without corrupting system integrity.","testable_assertion":"On January 1 of each year, any employee whose previous year-end balance exceeded 10 days has their balance set to 10. Employees with 10 or fewer days retain their full balance."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked a complete calendar month","when":"The accrual process runs for that month","then":"The employee's leave balance increases by exactly 1.5 days"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 2 days","when":"They submit a leave request for 3 days","then":"The request is rejected with an insufficient balance error and the balance remains 2 days"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 5 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request with a start date 2 working days from now","then":"The request is rejected with an insufficient advance notice error and the balance remains 5 days"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 5 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request with a start date exactly 3 working days from now","then":"The request is accepted and enters a pending approval state"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An employee was sick 3 calendar days ago","when":"They submit a medical leave request for that date with a valid doctor's note attached","then":"The request is accepted and enters a pending approval state"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An employee was sick 6 calendar days ago","when":"They submit a medical leave request for that date with a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected because it exceeds the 5-day retroactive submission window"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An employee was sick 3 calendar days ago","when":"They submit a medical leave request for that date without a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected because a doctor's note is required for retroactive medical leave"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A manager reviews a pending leave request","when":"The manager approves the request","then":"The employee's leave balance is reduced by the number of requested leave days"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A manager reviews a pending leave request","when":"The manager rejects the request","then":"The employee's leave balance remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An employee's contract ends on March 15","when":"They submit a leave request for March 14–16","then":"The request is rejected because it spans beyond the contract end date"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An employee's contract ends on March 15","when":"They submit a leave request for March 13–14","then":"The request is accepted (all dates are before the contract end date)"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"An employee has 14 days of unused leave on December 31","when":"The year-end carry-over process runs","then":"Their balance on January 1 is set to 10 days and 4 days are forfeited"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"An employee has 8 days of unused leave on December 31","when":"The year-end carry-over process runs","then":"Their balance on January 1 remains 8 days"},{"id":"AC-14","given":"An employee submits a leave request that passes all validation","when":"The request is still in pending state (not yet approved)","then":"The employee's leave balance is not yet reduced — balance is only consumed upon approval"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Employee submits leave request on the exact same day they accrued new leave","expected_behavior":"The newly accrued balance should be available immediately. Accrual must be processed before or atomically with request validation."},{"scenario":"Medical leave request submitted on exactly the 5th calendar day after the leave date","expected_behavior":"Should be accepted — the 'within 5 days' boundary is inclusive."},{"scenario":"Leave request start date falls after a weekend/holiday cluster, testing the 3 working day calculation","expected_behavior":"Working days exclude weekends and company holidays. A Friday request for a Monday start (no holidays) is only 1 working day of notice — rejected."},{"scenario":"Employee submits a leave request, then their contract end date is moved earlier before approval","expected_behavior":"The system should re-validate against the current contract end date at approval time, not just submission time. Otherwise an approved leave could violate BR-7."},{"scenario":"Multiple pending leave requests whose combined days exceed the balance, but each individually fits","expected_behavior":"Spec is silent on this. Recommendation: pending-but-not-yet-approved requests should reserve (earmark) balance to prevent over-commitment. This needs clarification."},{"scenario":"Year-end carry-over runs while leave requests are still pending approval","expected_behavior":"Spec is silent. Must decide whether pending requests count against the carry-over calculation or not. Needs clarification."},{"scenario":"Employee's contract end date is exactly the same as a requested leave day","expected_behavior":"Per BR-7, leave dates must not fall ON or after the contract end date. The contract end date itself is not a valid leave day."},{"scenario":"Employee with 0 balance submits a 0-day leave request","expected_behavior":"A 0-day leave request is semantically meaningless and should be rejected as invalid input regardless of balance."},{"scenario":"Partial month worked (e.g., employee starts mid-month)","expected_behavior":"Spec says 'per month worked' — unclear whether partial months accrue pro-rata (e.g., 0.75 days for half a month) or require full months. Needs clarification."},{"scenario":"Retroactive medical leave that spans multiple days, some within and some outside the 5-day window","expected_behavior":"Unclear whether the 5-day window applies to each individual leave day or the first/last day of the leave period. Needs clarification."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Leave balance is a financial liability on the company's books. Incorrect accrual or carry-over directly affects compensation expenses. Negative balance errors equate to unpaid labor disputes. Forfeiture errors could expose the company to legal claims.","operational":"Managers rely on accurate leave data for staffing and resource planning. Incorrect advance notice enforcement disrupts scheduling. Contract boundary violations create compliance gaps in termination workflows.","user":"Employees expect transparent, predictable leave balances. Rejected requests with unclear reasons erode trust. Retroactive medical leave is a sensitive UX moment — getting it wrong frustrates employees during illness recovery.","risk":"Labor law compliance risk if accrual, forfeiture, or balance rules violate local employment regulations (which vary by jurisdiction). Audit risk if approved leave exceeds accrued balance. Litigation risk if forfeiture is applied incorrectly.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Direct impact on compensation and work-life balance. Errors in balance or forfeiture affect earned benefits."},{"group":"Managers","impact":"Rely on leave system for approval workflows and team staffing visibility. Incorrect data leads to understaffing or approval errors."},{"group":"HR/People Operations","impact":"Own policy enforcement and audit compliance. Must trust system to correctly encode accrual, carry-over, and contract boundaries."},{"group":"Finance/Payroll","impact":"Leave balance is a financial liability. Year-end carry-over and forfeiture directly affect accrued liability reporting."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Must ensure leave policies comply with local labor laws. System must produce auditable records of accrual, consumption, and forfeiture."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is well-structured with clear rules for the core happy paths. However, several operationally important details are unspecified: partial month accrual, concurrent pending request handling, carry-over interaction with pending requests, and the exact boundary semantics for the retroactive medical leave window across multi-day requests. These gaps could lead to incorrect implementations that appear to satisfy the spec but fail in production. No jurisdictional labor law context was provided, which is a significant gap for a leave management system.","high_confidence_areas":["Non-negative balance invariant","Accrual rate and mechanism","Advance notice requirement for non-medical leave","Rejection does not consume balance","Contract end date boundary for leave requests","Year-end carry-over cap at 10 days"],"low_confidence_areas":["Partial month accrual calculation (pro-rata vs full months only)","Concurrent pending request balance reservation strategy","Multi-day retroactive medical leave window calculation","When exactly balance is consumed (at approval vs at submission)","Whether carry-over considers pending requests","Jurisdictional labor law compliance requirements"],"assumptions":["The 5-day retroactive window for medical leave is measured in calendar days, not working days","Contract end date means the employee's last working day + 1 (i.e., leave on the contract end date itself is invalid)","Leave balance is consumed at approval time, not at submission time","The 3 working day rule uses the company's holiday calendar, not just weekends","Year-end carry-over is a batch process that runs on or shortly after January 1","Accrual of 1.5 days applies to full months — partial month handling is undefined"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Partial month accrual is undefined. The spec states '1.5 days per month worked' but does not specify whether partial months (start/end of employment mid-month) accrue pro-rata or require a complete month.","affected_stakeholders":["Employees","HR/People Operations","Finance/Payroll"],"options":["Pro-rata accrual (e.g., 0.75 days for half a month worked)","Full month required (no accrual for partial months)","Round up — any days worked in a month count as a full month"],"recommendation":"Pro-rata accrual is the most employee-friendly and legally defensible approach. Recommend confirming with HR policy owner."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Concurrent pending leave requests may collectively exceed the available balance even though each individually fits. The spec does not address whether pending (unapproved) requests should reserve balance.","affected_stakeholders":["Employees","Managers"],"options":["Earmark balance at submission time — pending requests reduce available balance for new requests","No earmarking — balance only consumed at approval, allowing potential over-commitment that managers must resolve","Warn but allow — soft earmark with a warning to the employee"],"recommendation":"Earmark at submission time to prevent over-commitment. This is the safest approach and avoids forcing managers to arbitrate between conflicting approved requests."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Multi-day retroactive medical leave window semantics are unclear. If an employee was sick Monday through Wednesday and submits on the following Monday, is the 5-day window measured from the first sick day, the last sick day, or each individual day?","affected_stakeholders":["Employees","HR/People Operations"],"options":["5-day window measured from the last day of the leave period","5-day window measured from each individual leave day (some days may be outside the window)","5-day window measured from the first day of the leave period"],"recommendation":"Measure from the last day of the medical leave period. This is the most practical interpretation — an employee recovering from illness should not be penalized for multi-day absences."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
